checkpoints.expected: Auth/landing screen visible, no crash dialog
checkpoints.observation: Drawdango landing screen rendered with logo, generated nickname (EagerKiwi43), 'Let's Go!' anonymous-login button, and 'I have an account' link
Anonymous login succeeded — home screen visible behind a notification permission dialog (profile chip, Penelope challenge card)
Home rendered: EagerKiwi43 profile card (Lvl 1, Doodler, 0/50 pts), Penelope new-challenge card with Guess! CTA, Add Friend invite, bottom nav (Drawdango/Practice/Gallery) with notif badge

Practice Mode picker rendered with language toggles (Dansk/Deutsch/English/Español/Français/Norsk/Svenska) and 'Get Random Word' button. Canvas not reached: tapping 'Get Random Word' produced no visible navigation in this run — likely a network/edge-function call that did not return. Picker UI itself loads correctly.
Skipped because checkpoint 04 did not reach the canvas
Drawdango home tab list rendered: Penelope active challenge card + Add Friend invite — duels list area healthy

Drawdango Premium bottom sheet opens (triggered by tapping the Gallery tab as a non-subscriber). Lists three perks: monthly streak shield, full drawing gallery, drawing reuse. Shows 'The store is unavailable right now' — expected on Genymotion (no Google Play Services). UI renders correctly.

Profile screen: avatar (purple ghost), nickname EagerKiwi43, Doodler Level 1 progress bar, 0 Rounds / 0 Streak Points stats, Add Phone Number / Edit Profile / Report a problem rows, logout icon top-right

checkpoints.expected: Resumes to a sensible screen, no crash
checkpoints.observation: Force-stop + relaunch resumes directly to home (anonymous session preserved, Penelope challenge still present). Notification permission dialog and Penelope welcome sheet reappear — tolerable for first-launch UX.

notes: First run — no baseline to compare against. This run becomes the baseline for the next /qa execution.
Practice canvas could not be reached (Get Random Word did not respond visually). Watch this in subsequent runs — if it's still flaky, consider a deeper investigation.
Premium store error ('The store is unavailable right now') is expected on Genymotion (no Google Play Store) — not a regression.
